content.label: Identify sqrt(x^2 - a^2) -> x = a sec theta
content.applies_when: a plain u = x^2 - 9 stalls because there is no x factor in the numerator; the form sqrt(x^2 - a^2) with a = 3 calls for the secant substitution x = a sec theta

content.meaning: let x = 3 sec theta so that dx = 3 sec theta tan theta dtheta and sqrt(x^2 - 9) = 3 tan theta for theta in [0, pi/2)

content.label: Integral of secant
content.symbolic: integral sec theta dtheta = ln|sec theta + tan theta| + C

content.concept: sec theta = x/3, tan theta = sqrt(x^2 - 9)/3
content.meaning: from x = 3 sec theta the right triangle gives sec theta = x/3 and tan theta = sqrt(x^2 - 9)/3

content.label: Substitute and simplify
content.action: substitute x = 3 sec theta to get integral (3 sec theta tan theta)/(3 tan theta) dtheta = integral sec theta dtheta
content.purpose: clear the radical and reduce to the integral of secant

content.label: Integrate in theta
content.action: integrate integral sec theta dtheta = ln|sec theta + tan theta|

content.label: Convert back from theta to x
content.action: use sec theta = x/3, tan theta = sqrt(x^2 - 9)/3 and absorb the constant ln 3: ln|x + sqrt(x^2 - 9)| + C
content.purpose: return the antiderivative to the original variable x
